summary: Recent research on small UAS focuses on detection, tracking, and security challenges. One study introduces a lightweight neural network architecture for real-time detection of small UAVs, addressing issues of size, speed, and visual contrast. Another proposes a heterogeneous observer-follower framework to enable high-resolution UAV tracking on embedded platforms, resolving the trade-off between image resolution and processing speed. A third paper develops a multi-physics simulation framework for evaluating high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, aiming to neutralize autonomous drones without kinetic or RF jamming methods. Additionally, work on authenticated encryption frameworks targets secure telemetry transmission from resource-constrained IoT devices, including small drones, under adversarial wireless conditions. Finally, research on visual cooperative drone tracking integrates open-path gas sensors with UAVs for environmental monitoring, combining gas tomography with aerial mobility.
